details: The postinstall hook runs postinstall.js which collects the installer's external IP address, geo-location (city, region, country, ISP, organization), hostname, and username. This data is encoded into a DNS subdomain and exfiltrated via a DNS lookup to p1r2d74iwjk057raam6myf7e258wzkt8i[.]oastify[.]com, an attacker-controlled OAST callback host. The package also fetches geo-location data from ip-api[.]com/json/ during the exfiltration process. Both packages (gen-ai-opt-in and ai-gen-ai-opt-in) contain identical payloads with the same C2 domain. The package ai-gen-ai-opt-in@99.0.0 is a combosquat package with no repository or legitimate purpose. Its postinstall hook (postinstall.js) collects the installer's hostname, username, public IP address, city, region, country, ISP, and organization by querying ip-api[.]com/json/. All collected data is encoded into a subdomain and exfiltrated via a DNS lookup to the interact.sh callback host p1r2d74iwjk057raam6myf7e258wzkt8i[.]oastify[.]com, a DNS-tunnel exfiltration technique that bypasses HTTP-level monitoring.
